content: Date 2026-02-27. I'm deciding RLHF vs DPO for aligning our in-house 7B model for the Paris logistics assistant. Key facts: preference data ~2,400 labeled pairs collected Jan–Feb 2026 from 12 annotators; DPO explicitly skips the reward model (no RM training). With small pref data, simplicity might win. Current constraints: 4 A100s available, compute budget ~8 GPU-days, risk tolerance low (can't regress prod). Need a decision in two weeks (by 2026-03-12). Thinking out loud: RLHF pipeline = train reward model on prefs → PPO fine-tune. RM training adds complexity (calibration, overfit risk) and extra compute (~2–4 GPU-days) before PPO (~2–4 more). DPO = direct preference optimization, fewer moving parts, likely faster (est. 12–24h). But I'm worried DPO could underfit the 20% of prefs that are routing/factual rather than tone. Metrics I care about: validation win-rate on human eval (400 A/B), no regressions on production critical intents, and deterministic behavior for 3-month support. Baseline win-rate unknown yet — need to measure. Plan sketch: up-front measurement, then DPO first on the prefs; if DPO gives +8 points win-rate and no regressions, ship. Otherwise run RLHF. Keep all numbers and dates.
What's the current baseline win-rate on your 400 A/B human eval set?

Baseline is 48% win-rate vs editor reference on the 400 A/B set, measured 2026-02-20. Ops says ~80% of preferences are tone/clarity, ~20% are factual routing. Preference dataset: 2,400 pairs, 12 annotators, mean annotator disagreement 22%. Production constraints: 4 A100s, budget 8 GPU-days. Failure criteria: any regression that causes incorrect routing for high-priority shipments triggers immediate rollback. I'm thinking reserve 400 prefs for validation (400 matches our human eval), leaving 2,000 for training. Timeline: experiment and decide by 2026-03-12. If DPO underperforms on routing, follow with targeted RLHF.
Can you reserve the 400 prefs for validation and run both methods on the same 2,000 training prefs for a fair compare?

Yes — reserve 400 for val (same as the human eval set), 2,000 for training. Experimental plan: run DPO and RLHF in parallel if possible. Hypotheses: DPO converges faster and will improve tone (most prefs), RLHF can better shape routing but needs a well-generalized RM (which may not happen with only 2k prefs). Concrete schedule: DPO run first (est. 12–24h), RM training using 2k via cross-val (~1–2 days), then PPO for 24–48h if RM looks reasonable. Acceptance metrics: validation win-rate target = baseline +8 pts (i.e., >=56%), no >0.5% absolute drop on critical intent accuracy from production baseline measured 2026-02-10, and zero routing failures on high-priority cases. If DPO meets win-rate and safety, ship. Otherwise, plan to augment with +1,000 routing-focused prefs and run RLHF by 2026-03-26.
What's your threshold for acceptable regression on logs — absolute % or relative? For critical intents specifically?

Set threshold = no more than 0.5 percentage-point absolute drop on critical intent accuracy versus the production baseline (baseline measured 2026-02-10). Also: no single misrouting on high-priority shipments in a 24h simulated batch of 1,000 such cases. If either threshold is exceeded, immediate rollback and investigate. Augmentation plan for routing prefs: ops labeling sprint to collect +1,000 routing-focused pairs in two 3-day sprints with 6 annotators (83 pairs/annotator/day) at €3,000 budget. Quality gates: gold-set of 200 pairs, require >85% agreement per annotator; fallback to expert review by 2 senior PMs if gates fail.
How will you run the safety sim that checks for misrouting in the 1,000 high-priority case batch — synthetic deterministic tests or sampled recent production logs?

We'll run both: deterministic test suite of 200 canonical high-priority scenarios (created 2026-02-15) plus a sampled 1,000 recent production queries from Feb 2026. Log all model outputs for 14 days post-deploy and run pairwise comparisons on 5k recent production queries. Rollout policy: if DPO passes criteria, do a canary to 5% traffic for 48h, monitor the thresholds; full roll after 7 days with no regressions. If DPO fails on routing but improves tone, trigger the +1k routing prefs sprint and targeted RLHF, finish by 2026-03-26.

Summary to confirm: DPO first with 2k train + 400 val; acceptance = +8 win-rate (>=56%), <=0.5pp critical-intent drop, zero misroutes in 1,000 high-priority sample; canary 5% for 48h then full roll. If fail on routing, collect +1k routing prefs and run RLHF by 2026-03-26. Ready to schedule experiments?
